title: Capital One (2019) — Cloud Breach, Regulatory Enforcement, and Class Settlement
forum: Bank regulators + civil class action + criminal prosecution

timeline.event: Breach period begins (regulator describes March 2019 incident period)
Public disclosure of the incident (widely reported; company incident page context)
Federal Reserve announces cease and desist order
Final approval of class action settlement (settlement administrator site)
Settlement payments begin issuing (settlement administrator site)
Ninth Circuit opinion in U.S. v. Thompson (PDF opinion)

primary_sources.label: OCC press release: $80M civil money penalty
Federal Reserve press release: enforcement action
Federal Reserve cease and desist order (PDF)
Capital One class action settlement administrator site
DOJ case page: United States v. Paige Thompson
Ninth Circuit opinion: U.S. v. Thompson (PDF)

evidence_artifacts: Cloud security architecture + threat model for internet-facing apps
WAF configuration baselines + change management records
IAM policies/roles for cloud resources + least-privilege evidence
Metadata service protection controls evidence (where applicable) + test results
Centralized logging (auth, WAF, cloud audit) + retention policy + sample queries
Vulnerability management evidence (tickets, SLA, remediation metrics)
Risk assessments and documented risk acceptances (with approvers)
Internal audit / second-line reviews and follow-up remediation tracking
Incident response runbooks + tabletop records + post-incident lessons learned
engineering_takeaways: Treat cloud control-plane + WAF configuration as code: baseline, review, CI checks, drift detection.
Harden against SSRF-style paths to credential sources/metadata and enforce least privilege for cloud roles.
Prove governance: maintain auditable risk decisions, follow-up remediation, and independent testing evidence.

uncertainty: Some technical root-cause details differ between public reporting and formal orders; case page labels technical assertions by source type.
